title: Uruguayan-Style Empanada Filling with Reblochon
description: A savory empanada filling featuring the rich flavors of Uruguayan mustardy sausage and creamy reblochon cheese.

ingredients.name: Uruguayan sausage
ingredients.notes: Mustardy sausage can be found in most Latin American markets.

ingredients.name: Reblochon cheese
ingredients.notes: A semi-soft, creamy French cheese that pairs perfectly with the sausage.

ingredients.name: Gooey cheese
ingredients.notes: A mild, stretchy cheese that adds a nice texture to the filling.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Caramelized onions add a sweet, depth of flavor to the filling.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic and sauté it with the onions for added flavor.

ingredients.name: Salt and pepper
ingredients.notes: Season the filling with salt and pepper to taste.

ingredients.name: Empanada dough
ingredients.notes: Homemade empanada dough can be made from scratch, but store-bought is a convenient alternative.

ingredients.name: Egg, beaten
ingredients.notes: Brush the egg on the empanada dough before baking for a golden glaze.

instructions: In a large skillet, sauté the sliced sausage and diced onion over medium heat until the sausage is browned and the onion is caramelized.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Stir in the crumbled Reblochon cheese and shredded gooey cheese until melted and well combined.
Season the filling with salt and pepper to taste.
Roll out the empanada dough and cut out circles using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a spoonful of the filling in the center of each circle and brush the edges with a beaten egg.
Fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with additional beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
